results.title: Expand Energy AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Expand Energy Corporation has positioned itself strategically to capitalize on the growing AI-driven energy demand while leveraging advanced data and AI technologies in its own operations. The company, formed through a merger between Chesapeake Energy and Southwestern Energy in October 2024, is now the largest natural gas producer in the US[1]. Expand's leadership has emphasized their use of AI and machine learning for improving drilling efficiency and operational performance[2]. The company utilizes Snowflake's data platform to enable self-service analytics and deploy machine learning across 3,700+ production sites, allowing them to unify data and accelerate decision-making[3]. With significant exposure to AI data center power demand through their strategically located assets, Expand is well-positioned to benefit from the projected 20 billion cubic feet per day growth in natural gas demand through 2030[4].
...

AI skills are present but limited across Expand Energy, with 5.5% of all job postings mentioning AI. Tech roles show higher AI expectations at 11.1%, while non-tech roles show minimal AI requirements at 3.6%. The company's AI focus appears concentrated in technical and product development roles.

AI-powered drilling rate optimization and equipment failure prevention

Uses real-time data and ML models for drilling activities to optimize rate of penetration, prevent equipment failures and enhance safety, building on foundation of real-time data ingestion
...
How is Expand Energy using AI to improve drilling operations?
...
Expand Energy uses AI and machine learning to support record-breaking drilling performance, optimize drilling rates, prevent equipment failures, and enhance safety across all their producing regions.
...
The company uses Snowflake's platform to unify data across 3,700+ production sites, enabling self-service analytics and ML deployment that has resulted in 40% M&A efficiency gains and significant labor savings.

I’m thrilled to announce that I’ve officially started my career as a Physical Natural Gas Trading Analyst at Expand Energy , the largest natural gas producer in North America! 

I’m incredibly grateful for this opportunity to dive into the energy markets and learn from some of the best in the industry. Looking forward to this next chapter and contributing to the future of energy!
...
Activity: Brendan
...
This time last year I posted about it being my last year celebrating a work anniversary with Chesapeake/Expand Energy. My last day was set to be May 2nd. Thankfully, a week before May 2nd I was asked to stay on (many emotions!). I was so thankful for that ask, but I also felt so guilty for those who didn’t have that same opportunity (survivors remorse). So I didn’t post about it. But here we are a year later.  Now getting to celebrate year 23. Officially half my life has been working at Chesapeake now Expand Energy. 
It has been a crazy and wild ride but am so very thankful to get celebrate another year!

results.title: Senior AI/ML Engineer at Jobgether - United State | Visa Sponsorship Jobs
highlights:
# Senior AI/ML Engineer
...
We are seeking a highly skilled AI/ML Engineer to develop advanced AI and ML solutions for the energy and utility sector. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making. The role requires collaboration with cross-functional teams to design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems.
...
Develop AI and ML solutions for the energy and utility sector
...
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and deploy scalable AI and machine learning solutions integrated into production systems
...
Build reusable Python libraries and engineering components to support ML pipelines, data processing workflows, and AI system development
...
Python AWS Azure GCP Databricks LangChain LangGraph LlamaIndex Hugging Face AutoGen
...
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for an AI / ML Engineer in the United States.This role sits at the intersection of machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making, focused on solving complex challenges in the energy and utility sector. You will contribute to the development of advanced AI and ML solutions that combine predictive modeling, optimization, and generative AI to support critical infrastructure and sustainability goals. Working closely with data scientists and software engineers, you will help design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. The environment is highly collaborative and innovation-driven, with a strong emphasis on applied AI, production readiness, and measurable impact. You will work across diverse use cases including forecasting, geospatial analysis, and intelligent decision systems. This is a high-impact role where your work directly supports the modernization and efficiency of energy systems.Accountabilities
...
- Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design, develop, and deploy scalable AI and machine learning solutions integrated into production systems.
...
- Build reusable Python libraries and engineering components to support ML pipelines, data processing workflows, and AI system development.
...
- Contribute to the design and implementation of modern AI architectures, including RAG systems, LLM integrations, and agent-based workflows.
...
- Develop frameworks for
...
- Support database design and optimization, including writing efficient queries for large-scale data processing and retrieval.
...
- Stay current with advancements in machine learning engineering, generative AI, and AI orchestration, integrating best practices into workflows.

highlights:
Energy Data Strategist (Staff/ Sr Staff) | Jobflarely
...
# Energy Data Strategist (Staff/ Sr Staff)
...
Equilibrium Energy is a team of technologists, power market experts, and AI pioneers reimagining how the world’s most critical industry operates. We’re building a first-of-its-kind AI operating system for the power sector, uniting cutting-edge science with real-world purpose to enable a cleaner, more resilient energy future. At EQ, you’ll join a tight-knit group of brilliant, curious, and adventurous people who bring the same energy to collaboration as they do to innovation.
...
Series B clean energy startup
...
the most prominent institutional investors in climate
...
the ground up on deep industry expertise combined with an unwavering commitment to modern digital approaches
...
passionate and resourceful folks to join our team
...
foundation for our
...
mission and ambitious plan
...
Data is central to the success of the Equilibrium Energy mission. The data managed by the Data Services team is incredibly robust ranging from telemetry outputs to inputs into machine learning models and includes public, proprietary and private data points. All of this needs to be centralized, synthesized and governed to ensure its secure usability across our various teams. The role of the Energy Analyst is to advance our data system and platform so it will empower our teams to better utilize our market data warehouse. The Energy Data Strategist will do this by applying domain and market expertise to advance our raw data into explorable, understandable data views and processes. The ideal candidate will have a passion for energy markets, data engineering and analytics.
...
- Engineering and Analytics
- Work closely with data engineers to define data ingestion requirements
- Define, refine and apply our standard data model
- Capture data utilization requirements from end-users
- Develop end-user tables within Databricks utilizing SQL and DBT
- Seek out and collect feedback to improve our data life-cycle
- Create information that supports our semantic layer and data ontology

- Data Support and Quality Assurance
- Define and manage quality assurance processes in our data layers
- Research market data, review technology forums and monitor market publications to ensure we are future proof to new products and changes in reporting requirements
...
- BS/Master's degree in a quantitative discipline (e.g., Computer Science, Operations Research, Industrial Engineering, Mathematics, Finance, Economics, Physics, Electrical Engineering) or equivalent practical experience.
...
- 7+ years experience with energy market data, including nodal power market results, forecasting, and market research.
- Demonstrated experience with SQL and Python; ability to translate raw data into clean market views that allows the business to analyze market data.
...
- Demonstrated experience in DBT and Databricks
- Deep expertise with Business intelligence tools, including Sigma, Hex, PowerBI, or Tableau.
- Experience communicating the results of analyses with product, engineering, and leadership teams to influence product and engineering strategy.
...
Equilibrium is composed of deeply knowledgeable industry experts across all our functions, with decades of experience in energy-specific commercial structuring, power systems engineering, machine learning, computational research, operations research, distributed and compute-intensive infrastructure, and modern software & ML engineering. Our experience in the space means we’ve previously built versions of nearly every technical component of our platform. We are now designing them better, and combining them in a holistic and novel way, to achieve global scale and climate impact. We pride ourselves on our deeply empathetic & collaborative culture, honest and direct but respectful communication, and our balanced, flexible, and remote-first work environment.

results.title: Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist) | Wfh
highlights:
Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist) | Wfh
...
# Data Scientist (AI Data & LLM Specialist)
...
Join the core team at Eclipse, where we’re building an AI agent-first marketplace that connects intelligence with real-world tasks, starting with data collection and labeling. We are seeking a Data Scientist to establish the foundation for how our data is labeled, processed, and prepared for consumption by next-generation Large Language Models (LLMs). Your work will be critical in transforming our raw data collections into valuable, AI-ready datasets.
...
- Proven experience as a Data Scientist or Machine Learning Engineer with a focus on data quality and preparation.
- Strong understanding of data labeling methodologies and hands-on experience with data annotation platforms and workflows.
...
- Demonstrated experience preparing datasets for training and fine-tuning Large Language Models (LLMs), including knowledge of techniques like tokenization, embeddings, and NER.
...
- Proficiency in Python and common data science libraries (e.g., Pandas, NumPy, Scikit-learn, spaCy, Hugging Face).
...
- Experience using APIs/SDKs to automate data annotation and active learning loops.
...
- Responsibilities
...
- Develop Data Labeling Strategies: Design and document a formal data annotation strategy, including clear, scalable, and efficient guidelines for labeling our data. Define and enforce quality metrics, including inter-annotator agreement.
...
- Optimize for LLM Consumption: Research, define, and prototype the optimal data formats, structures, and pre-processing steps required for fine-tuning and training LLMs on our datasets.
...
- Data Quality Analysis: Establish automated processes and metrics to analyze the quality of both raw and labeled data, providing feedback to improve our data collection and labeling workflows.
...
- Collaborate with Engineering: Work closely with the engineering team to guide the implementation of data processing pipelines and ensure the data infrastructure meets the needs of ML applications.
...
- Nice-to-Haves
- Experience with audio data processing and relevant libraries.
- Familiarity with data annotation platforms and tools.
- Knowledge of modern MLOps principles and practices.
- Experience with large language model data curation and Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF) pipelines.
